old_value: {"id":"feynfeld-43a.1","title":"Design Pair type system for Julia","description":"FeynCalc's Pair[x,y] encodes 3 things via argument heads: metric (LI,LI), four-vector (LI,Mom), scalar product (Mom,Mom). In Julia: decide whether to use one Pair type with canonical ordering + inner constructor (like FeynCalc) or separate types. Must handle Orderless (canonical arg ordering). Must integrate with TensorGR's TProduct. Research FeynCalc SharedObjects.m lines 1-300.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-26T11:01:41Z","created_by":"tobias","updated_at":"2026-03-26T11:01:41Z"}

new_value: Design spike complete: hybrid approach chosen (universal Pair type from FeynCalc + SPContext from Julia-idiomatic design + fold/reduce contract). Three agents ran: research, Design A, Design B.
Momentum type implemented in src/algebra/momentum.jl with DimSlot support, ordering, hash. 14 tests.
ScalarProduct mechanism implemented: Pair(Momentum,Momentum) replaces old ScalarProduct struct, SPContext for value storage, SP/SPD/SPE convenience constructors. 33 tests for Pair + 10 for SPContext.
{"assignee":"tobiasosborne","status":"in_progress"}
old_value: {"id":"feynfeld-43a.5","title":"Implement Contract (Lorentz index contraction)","description":"Port FeynCalc Contract. THE core operation. Algorithm: FCSplit to separate indexed/free parts → DummyIndexFreeQ to filter → tree-walk to find repeated indices → substitute. In FeynCalc uses PairContract trick (UpValues for eager contraction). In Julia: explicit tree-walking pass via TensorGR's contract_metrics or a dedicated contract() function. Must handle: metric contraction g^μν g_νρ, four-vector contraction p^μ g_μν, BMHV mixed-dim contractions. Ref: Lorentz/Contract.m (718 lines), PairContract.m (427 lines).","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-26T11:01:43Z","created_by":"tobias","updated_at":"2026-03-26T11:01:43Z"}

new_value: Contract implemented in src/algebra/contract.jl: worklist reducer for metric-metric, metric-vector, vector-vector contractions. Full BMHV coverage (all 9x3 dim combinations). SPContext integration. Einstein violation detection. 46 contract tests.
{"assignee":"tobiasosborne","status":"in_progress"}
old_value: {"id":"feynfeld-43a.4","title":"Implement ExpandScalarProduct","description":"Port FeynCalc ExpandScalarProduct: expand Pair[Momentum[a+b], Momentum[c]] → Pair[Momentum[a],Momentum[c]] + Pair[Momentum[b],Momentum[c]]. Uses linearity, NOT Mathematica's Expand. Finds tensor objects containing Plus and expands individually. Ref: Lorentz/ExpandScalarProduct.m (important: does NOT use Mathematica Expand).","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-26T11:01:43Z","created_by":"tobias","updated_at":"2026-03-26T11:01:43Z"}

old_value: {"id":"feynfeld-mpw.1","title":"Implement DiracGamma type with dimension tagging","description":"Port FeynCalc DiracGamma. Encodes: γ^μ (DiracGamma[LorentzIndex[μ]]), γ^5, γ^6=(1+γ^5)/2, γ^7=(1-γ^5)/2, slashed momenta (DiracGamma[Momentum[p]]). Dimension rules: DiracGamma[x,4]:=DiracGamma[x], DiracGamma[0,___]:=0, γ^5/6/7 always 4D, 4D object in (D-4) dims = 0. Mark as NonCommutative. Ref: SharedObjects.m DiracGamma section.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-26T11:02:27Z","created_by":"tobias","updated_at":"2026-03-26T11:02:27Z"}

old_value: {"id":"feynfeld-mpw.3","title":"Implement DiracTrick (core Dirac simplification rules)","description":"THE HARDEST FILE. Port FeynCalc DiracTrick.m (2102 lines). Core rules: γ^μ.γ_μ=D, γ^μ.γ^ν.γ_μ=(2-D)γ^ν, Clifford algebra {γ^μ,γ^ν}=2g^{μν}, commutation of γ^5/6/7 through chains, Dirac equation (p̸-m).u(p)=0, BMHV splitting. Uses sequence pattern matching (x___,γ^5,y___) → in Julia: explicit position-based iteration over chain elements. This will need multiple research rounds.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-26T11:02:28Z","created_by":"tobias","updated_at":"2026-03-26T11:02:28Z"}

new_value: DiracTrick core rules in dirac_trick.jl: gamma5 squared, projector algebra (g6/g7), slash squared, adjacent trace (g^mu g_mu = D), sandwich contraction n=1 (g^mu g^a g_mu = (2-D)g^a) and n=2. 35 new tests, 352 total.
{"assignee":"tobiasosborne","status":"in_progress"}
old_value: {"id":"feynfeld-mpw.5","title":"Implement DiracTrace (trace evaluation)","description":"Port FeynCalc DiracTrace.m (775 lines). Key: trace is a HEAD that wraps expressions, evaluated only when DiracTraceEvaluate→True. Without γ^5: standard recursive formula. With γ^5: scheme-dependent (NDR, BMHV, Larin). CRITICAL: Tr with single γ^5 in NDR is LEFT UNEVALUATED (genuine physical ambiguity). Must support 4 γ^5 schemes. TraceOfOne default=4. Ref: Dirac/DiracTrace.m.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-26T11:02:30Z","created_by":"tobias","updated_at":"2026-03-26T11:02:30Z"}

new_value: DiracTrace in dirac_trace.jl: recursive trace formula for non-chiral (Tr[g^a1...g^{2n}] = Σ (-1)^i g^{a1,ai} Tr[rest]), chiral base case (Tr[g5 g^a g^b g^c g^d] = -4 ε^{abcd}), g5 parity handling. 15 new tests, 367 total.

old_value: {"id":"feynfeld-sem.1","title":"Implement SU(N) type system (SUNT, SUNF, SUND, SUNDelta, indices)","description":"Port FeynCalc colour types. SUNT[a] = generator T^a (NonCommutative, lives in DOT chains). SUNTF[{a},i,j] = T^a with explicit fundamental indices. SUNF[a,b,c] = structure constants f^{abc}. SUND[a,b,c] = symmetric d^{abc} (Orderless). SUNDelta[a,b] = δ^{ab} adjoint. SUNFDelta[a,b] = δ^{ab} fundamental. SUNIndex[a] vs SUNFIndex[a]. CA=N, CF=(N²-1)/(2N). Ref: SUN/ types in SharedObjects.m.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-26T11:02:54Z","created_by":"tobias","updated_at":"2026-03-26T11:02:54Z"}
